$comment: First-party curated marketplace catalog (CP-FR-020 issue #621; CP-FR-021 issue #622; per-plugin subdir sources + signing-key rotation, issue #750). The catalog is a SIGNED envelope: `signature` is a base64 detached ed25519 signature over the canonical bytes of `payload` (see server/services/marketplace-integrity.ts), verified at load against the bundled first-party public key. The server fails closed (zero listings) on any signature mismatch. Each entry's `source.directory` names the subdirectory of the cloned repo that holds the plugin package (the monorepo-subdir model, #750): the installer clones `source.url`, stages only that subdirectory, and installs it. Each entry carries an `integrity` content digest (sha256-<hex> over the staged subdirectory), checked after staging and before commit; `revoked: true` removes an entry from listings and blocks install/update. The three installable entries (database, process, github-com) carry REAL digests of their `plugins/<id>` subdirectory at the signed commit, validated end-to-end through the actual verifyPackageIntegrity path by a no-network CI test (server/services/marketplace-integrity.test.ts, issue #689). ghe and jira-self-hosted were extracted to the workplace marketplace and are no longer first-party (davidpoxon/roubo-development#568); the unpublished `redis` placeholder and the withdrawn `worker-queue` placeholder were removed. Because a digest binds to live `plugins/<id>` content, any change to those directories requires recomputing the digest and RE-SIGNING this file (the #689 test fails closed on drift). `verified` is a display-only curation flag. There is no third-party submission path: entries are added by maintainers only. The signing key was rotated in #750 (the prior key was lost); the matching ed25519 private key is held out of band and never committed. After editing this file you MUST re-sign it (pipe the ed25519 private-key PEM in on stdin): npx tsx server/scripts/sign-marketplace-catalog.ts < /path/to/ed25519-priv.pem
